item.title: Subscribe
item.dc:creator: unipix-kids
item.content:encoded:
<input 
				type="email" 
				defaultValue="" 
				class="mf-input " 
				id="mf-input-email-0c8bd49" 
				name="mf-email" 
				placeholder="${ parent.decodeEntities(`Enter your mail...`) } " 
				onBlur=${parent.handleChange} onFocus=${parent.handleChange} aria-invalid=${validation.errors['mf-email'] ? 'true' : 'false' } 
				ref=${el=> parent.activateValidation({"message":"This field is required.","emailMessage":"Please enter a valid Email address","minLength":1,"maxLength":"","type":"none","required":false,"expression":"null"}, el)}
							/>
						<${validation.ErrorMessage} 
				errors=${validation.errors} 
				name="mf-email" 
				as=${html``}
			/>
							<button type="submit" id="">
					${ parent.decodeEntities(`Subscribe`) } 
									</button>

item.title: Admission form
item.dc:creator: unipix-kids
item.content:encoded:
<input type="text" id="mf-input-text-b19f3d7" 
				name="mf-listing-fname" 
				placeholder="${ parent.decodeEntities(`First Name`) } "
				onInput=${ parent.handleChange }
									aria-invalid=${validation.errors['mf-listing-fname'] ? 'true' : 'false'}
					ref=${ el => parent.activateValidation({"message":"This field is required.","minLength":1,"maxLength":"","type":"none","required":true,"expression":"null"}, el) }
							/>
							<${validation.ErrorMessage}
					errors=${validation.errors}
					name="mf-listing-fname"
					as=${html``}
					/>
			<input type="text" id="mf-input-text-80c2a8a" 
				name="mf-listing-lname" 
				placeholder="${ parent.decodeEntities(`Last Name`) } "
				onInput=${ parent.handleChange }	
									aria-invalid=${validation.errors['mf-listing-lname'] ? 'true' : 'false'}
					ref=${ el => parent.activateValidation({"message":"This field is required.","minLength":1,"maxLength":"","type":"none","required":true,"expression":"null"}, el) }
			/>
							<${validation.ErrorMessage}
					errors=${validation.errors}
					name="mf-listing-lname"
					as=${html``}
					/>
			<input 
				type="email" 
				defaultValue="" 
				class="mf-input " 
				id="mf-input-email-503de31" 
				name="mf-email" 
				placeholder="${ parent.decodeEntities(`Email`) } " 
				onBlur=${parent.handleChange} onFocus=${parent.handleChange} aria-invalid=${validation.errors['mf-email'] ? 'true' : 'false' } 
				ref=${el=> parent.activateValidation({"message":"This field is required.","emailMessage":"Please enter a valid Email address","minLength":1,"maxLength":"","type":"none","required":true,"expression":"null"}, el)}
							/>
						<${validation.ErrorMessage} 
				errors=${validation.errors} 
				name="mf-email" 
				as=${html``}
			/>
			<input
				type="tel"
				class="mf-input "
				id="mf-input-telephone-b08b757" 
				name="mf-telephone"
				placeholder="${ parent.decodeEntities(`Phone`) } "
									onInput=${parent.handleChange}
					aria-invalid=${validation.errors['mf-telephone'] ? 'true' : 'false'}
					ref=${el => parent.activateValidation({"message":"This field is required.","minLength":1,"maxLength":"","type":"none","required":true,"expression":"null"}, el)}
								/>
							<${validation.ErrorMessage}
					errors=${validation.errors}
					name="mf-telephone"
					as=${html``}
					/>
			<input
				type="text"
				class="mf-input "
				id="mf-input-text-842da34"
				name="mf-text"
				placeholder="${ parent.decodeEntities(`Address`) } "
									onInput=${parent.handleChange}
					onBlur=${parent.handleChange}
					aria-invalid=${validation.errors['mf-text'] ? 'true' : 'false'}
					ref=${el =>{
												parent.activateValidation({"message":"This field is required.","minLength":1,"maxLength":"","type":"none","required":false,"expression":"null"}, el)
					}}
								/>
							<${validation.ErrorMessage}
					errors=${validation.errors}
					name="mf-text"
					as=${html``}
					/>
			<input
				type="text"
				class="mf-input "
				id="mf-input-text-c4c129a"
				name="mf-text"
				placeholder="${ parent.decodeEntities(`City`) } "
									onInput=${parent.handleChange}
					onBlur=${parent.handleChange}
					aria-invalid=${validation.errors['mf-text'] ? 'true' : 'false'}
					ref=${el =>{
												parent.activateValidation({"message":"This field is required.","minLength":1,"maxLength":"","type":"none","required":true,"expression":"null"}, el)
					}}
								/>
							<${validation.ErrorMessage}
					errors=${validation.errors}
					name="mf-text"
					as=${html``}
					/>
			<input
				type="text"
				class="mf-input "
				id="mf-input-text-6620f32"
				name="mf-text"
				placeholder="${ parent.decodeEntities(`Country`) } "
									onInput=${parent.handleChange}
					onBlur=${parent.handleChange}
					aria-invalid=${validation.errors['mf-text'] ? 'true' : 'false'}
					ref=${el =>{
												parent.activateValidation({"message":"This field is required.","minLength":1,"maxLength":"","type":"none","required":true,"expression":"null"}, el)
					}}
								/>
							<${validation.ErrorMessage}
					errors=${validation.errors}
					name="mf-text"
					as=${html``}
					/>
			<input
				type="number"
				step="any"
				class="mf-input "
				id="mf-input-mobile-0a8340b"
				name="mf-number"
				placeholder="${ parent.decodeEntities(`Zip Code`) } "
									onInput=${parent.handleChange}
					aria-invalid=${validation.errors['mf-number'] ? 'true' : 'false'}
					ref=${el => parent.activateValidation({"message":"This field is required.","minLength":1,"maxLength":"","type":"none","required":true,"expression":"null"}, el)}
								/>
							<${validation.ErrorMessage}
					errors=${validation.errors}
					name="mf-number"
					as=${html``}
					/>
			<${props.Flatpickr}
					name="mf-date"
					className="mf-input mf-date-input mf-left-parent  "
					placeholder="${ parent.decodeEntities(`dd/mm/yy`) } "
					options=${{"minDate":"","maxDate":"","dateFormat":"m-d-Y","enableTime":"","disable":[],"mode":"single","static":true,"disableMobile":true,"time_24hr":false}}
					value=${parent.getValue('mf-date')}
					onInput=${parent.handleDateTime}
					aria-invalid=${validation.errors['mf-date'] ? 'true' : 'false'}
					ref=${el => props.DateWidget(
							el, 
							'', 
							{"message":"This field is required.","required":false},  
							register, 
							parent 
						)}
					/>
							<${validation.ErrorMessage}
					errors=${validation.errors}
					name="mf-date"
					as=${html``}
					/>
			<textarea id="mf-input-text-area-af9d55a"
				name="mf-textarea" 
				placeholder="${ parent.decodeEntities(`Enter your Comments`) } "
				cols="30" rows="10"
									onInput=${ parent.handleChange }
					aria-invalid=${validation.errors['mf-textarea'] ? 'true' : 'false'}
					ref=${ el => parent.activateValidation({"message":"This field is required.","minLength":1,"maxLength":"","type":"none","required":false,"expression":"null"}, el)}
								></textarea>
							<${validation.ErrorMessage}
					errors=${validation.errors}
					name="mf-textarea"
					as=${html``}
					/>
							<button type="submit" id="">
					${ parent.decodeEntities(`Apply Now`) } 
									</button>

item.title: Team Form
item.dc:creator: unipix-kids
item.content:encoded:
<input
				type="text"
				class="mf-input "
				id="mf-input-text-f12a5f9"
				name="mf-text"
				placeholder="${ parent.decodeEntities(`Your name`) } "
									onInput=${parent.handleChange}
					onBlur=${parent.handleChange}
					aria-invalid=${validation.errors['mf-text'] ? 'true' : 'false'}
					ref=${el =>{
												parent.activateValidation({"message":"This field is required.","minLength":1,"maxLength":"","type":"none","required":true,"expression":"null"}, el)
					}}
								/>
							<${validation.ErrorMessage}
					errors=${validation.errors}
					name="mf-text"
					as=${html``}
					/>
			<input 
				type="email" 
				defaultValue="" 
				class="mf-input " 
				id="mf-input-email-204b09f" 
				name="mf-email" 
				placeholder="${ parent.decodeEntities(`Your email`) } " 
				onBlur=${parent.handleChange} onFocus=${parent.handleChange} aria-invalid=${validation.errors['mf-email'] ? 'true' : 'false' } 
				ref=${el=> parent.activateValidation({"message":"This field is required.","emailMessage":"Please enter a valid Email address","minLength":1,"maxLength":"","type":"none","required":true,"expression":"null"}, el)}
							/>
						<${validation.ErrorMessage} 
				errors=${validation.errors} 
				name="mf-email" 
				as=${html``}
			/>
			<input
				type="tel"
				class="mf-input "
				id="mf-input-telephone-79d6a1f" 
				name="mf-telephone"
				placeholder="${ parent.decodeEntities(`Phone Number`) } "
									onInput=${parent.handleChange}
					aria-invalid=${validation.errors['mf-telephone'] ? 'true' : 'false'}
					ref=${el => parent.activateValidation({"message":"This field is required.","minLength":1,"maxLength":"","type":"none","required":false,"expression":"null"}, el)}
								/>
							<${validation.ErrorMessage}
					errors=${validation.errors}
					name="mf-telephone"
					as=${html``}
					/>
			<input
				type="text"
				class="mf-input "
				id="mf-input-text-eeaee98"
				name="mf-text"
				placeholder="${ parent.decodeEntities(`Subject`) } "
									onInput=${parent.handleChange}
					onBlur=${parent.handleChange}
					aria-invalid=${validation.errors['mf-text'] ? 'true' : 'false'}
					ref=${el =>{
												parent.activateValidation({"message":"This field is required.","minLength":1,"maxLength":"","type":"none","required":false,"expression":"null"}, el)
					}}
								/>
							<${validation.ErrorMessage}
					errors=${validation.errors}
					name="mf-text"
					as=${html``}
					/>
							<button type="submit" id="">
					${ parent.decodeEntities(`Send Message`) } 
				</button>

item.title: Contact Form
item.dc:creator: unipix-kids
item.content:encoded:
<label for="mf-input-text-01c509c">
					${ parent.decodeEntities(`Full name`) } 					*
				</label>
			<input
				type="text"
				class="mf-input "
				id="mf-input-text-01c509c"
				name="mf-text"
				placeholder="${ parent.decodeEntities(`Your name`) } "
									onInput=${parent.handleChange}
					onBlur=${parent.handleChange}
					aria-invalid=${validation.errors['mf-text'] ? 'true' : 'false'}
					ref=${el =>{
												parent.activateValidation({"message":"This field is required.","minLength":1,"maxLength":"","type":"none","required":true,"expression":"null"}, el)
					}}
								/>
							<${validation.ErrorMessage}
					errors=${validation.errors}
					name="mf-text"
					as=${html``}
					/>
							<label for="mf-input-email-5e7887e">
					${ parent.decodeEntities(`Email address`) } 					*
				</label>
			<input 
				type="email" 
				defaultValue="" 
				class="mf-input " 
				id="mf-input-email-5e7887e" 
				name="mf-email" 
				placeholder="${ parent.decodeEntities(`Your email`) } " 
				onBlur=${parent.handleChange} onFocus=${parent.handleChange} aria-invalid=${validation.errors['mf-email'] ? 'true' : 'false' } 
				ref=${el=> parent.activateValidation({"message":"This field is required.","emailMessage":"Please enter a valid Email address","minLength":1,"maxLength":"","type":"none","required":true,"expression":"null"}, el)}
							/>
						<${validation.ErrorMessage} 
				errors=${validation.errors} 
				name="mf-email" 
				as=${html``}
			/>
							<label for="mf-input-telephone-e3d8e3b">
					${ parent.decodeEntities(`Telephone`) } 					
				</label>
			<input
				type="tel"
				class="mf-input "
				id="mf-input-telephone-e3d8e3b" 
				name="mf-telephone"
				placeholder="${ parent.decodeEntities(`Phone Number`) } "
									onInput=${parent.handleChange}
					aria-invalid=${validation.errors['mf-telephone'] ? 'true' : 'false'}
					ref=${el => parent.activateValidation({"message":"This field is required.","minLength":1,"maxLength":"","type":"none","required":false,"expression":"null"}, el)}
								/>
							<${validation.ErrorMessage}
					errors=${validation.errors}
					name="mf-telephone"
					as=${html``}
					/>
							<label for="mf-input-text-83b8240">
					${ parent.decodeEntities(`Text`) } 					
				</label>
			<input
				type="text"
				class="mf-input "
				id="mf-input-text-83b8240"
				name="mf-text"
				placeholder="${ parent.decodeEntities(`Subject`) } "
									onInput=${parent.handleChange}
					onBlur=${parent.handleChange}
					aria-invalid=${validation.errors['mf-text'] ? 'true' : 'false'}
					ref=${el =>{
												parent.activateValidation({"message":"This field is required.","minLength":1,"maxLength":"","type":"none","required":false,"expression":"null"}, el)
					}}
								/>
							<${validation.ErrorMessage}
					errors=${validation.errors}
					name="mf-text"
					as=${html``}
					/>
							<label for="mf-input-text-area-1de3a4f">
					${ parent.decodeEntities(`How can we Help? `) } 					*
				</label>
			<textarea id="mf-input-text-area-1de3a4f"
				name="mf-textarea" 
				placeholder="${ parent.decodeEntities(`Your Message`) } "
				cols="30" rows="10"
									onInput=${ parent.handleChange }
					aria-invalid=${validation.errors['mf-textarea'] ? 'true' : 'false'}
					ref=${ el => parent.activateValidation({"message":"This field is required.","minLength":1,"maxLength":"","type":"none","required":true,"expression":"null"}, el)}
								></textarea>
							<${validation.ErrorMessage}
					errors=${validation.errors}
					name="mf-textarea"
					as=${html``}
					/>
							<button type="submit" id="">
					${ parent.decodeEntities(`Send Message`) } 
					<svg aria-hidden="true" viewBox="0 0 512 512" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><path d="M444.52 3.52L28.74 195.42c-47.97 22.39-31.98 92.75 19.19 92.75h175.91v175.91c0 51.17 70.36 67.17 92.75 19.19l191.9-415.78c15.99-38.39-25.59-79.97-63.97-63.97z"></path></svg>				</button>

item.title: Admission Form 2
item.dc:creator: unipix-kids
item.content:encoded:

item.title: Privacy Policy
item.dc:creator: unipix-kids
item.content:encoded:
Who we are

Suggested text: Our website address is: https://themewant.com/kids.

Comments

Suggested text: When visitors leave comments on the site we collect the data shown in the comments form, and also the visitor’s IP address and browser user agent string to help spam detection.

An anonymized string created from your email address (also called a hash) may be provided to the Gravatar service to see if you are using it. The Gravatar service privacy policy is available here: https://automattic.com/privacy/. After approval of your comment, your profile picture is visible to the public in the context of your comment.

Media

Suggested text: If you upload images to the website, you should avoid uploading images with embedded location data (EXIF GPS) included. Visitors to the website can download and extract any location data from images on the website.

Cookies

Suggested text: If you leave a comment on our site you may opt-in to saving your name, email address and website in cookies. These are for your convenience so that you do not have to fill in your details again when you leave another comment. These cookies will last for one year.

If you visit our login page, we will set a temporary cookie to determine if your browser accepts cookies. This cookie contains no personal data and is discarded when you close your browser.

When you log in, we will also set up several cookies to save your login information and your screen display choices. Login cookies last for two days, and screen options cookies last for a year. If you select "Remember Me", your login will persist for two weeks. If you log out of your account, the login cookies will be removed.

If you edit or publish an article, an additional cookie will be saved in your browser. This cookie includes no personal data and simply indicates the post ID of the article you just edited. It expires after 1 day.

Embedded content from other websites

Suggested text: Articles on this site may include embedded content (e.g. videos, images, articles, etc.). Embedded content from other websites behaves in the exact same way as if the visitor has visited the other website.

These websites may collect data about you, use cookies, embed additional third-party tracking, and monitor your interaction with that embedded content, including tracking your interaction with the embedded content if you have an account and are logged in to that website.

Who we share your data with

Suggested text: If you request a password reset, your IP address will be included in the reset email.

How long we retain your data

Suggested text: If you leave a comment, the comment and its metadata are retained indefinitely. This is so we can recognize and approve any follow-up comments automatically instead of holding them in a moderation queue.

For users that register on our website (if any), we also store the personal information they provide in their user profile. All users can see, edit, or delete their personal information at any time (except they cannot change their username). Website administrators can also see and edit that information.

What rights you have over your data

Suggested text: If you have an account on this site, or have left comments, you can request to receive an exported file of the personal data we hold about you, including any data you have provided to us. You can also request that we erase any personal data we hold about you. This does not include any data we are obliged to keep for administrative, legal, or security purposes.

Where your data is sent

Suggested text: Visitor comments may be checked through an automated spam detection service.

item.title: Why I Love Teaching Kindergarten Every Day
item.dc:creator: unipix-kids
item.content:encoded:
Teaching kindergarten is a journey filled with joy, surprises, and countless rewards. Every day brings new moments of curiosity, laughter, and discovery as young learners explore the world around them. As a teacher, witnessing their “aha” moments, encouraging their creativity, and guiding them through challenges is incredibly fulfilling. Each child brings unique perspectives, energy, and questions that inspire growth—not only for them but for me as well. From building friendships to developing early skills, the classroom is a vibrant space where learning meets play. Sharing these experiences highlights the beauty and impact of early education.

One of the most rewarding aspects of teaching kindergarten is seeing children develop confidence in themselves. Simple accomplishments, like learning to tie their shoes, sharing a toy with a friend, or confidently answering a question aloud, are milestones that reflect their growing independence. As a teacher, guiding students through these small but significant moments is incredibly fulfilling. Every smile, “aha” moment, and burst of excitement reinforces the importance of fostering a nurturing and encouraging environment.

Kindergarten is also a time when creativity flourishes. Whether it’s through art, storytelling, imaginative play, or hands-on projects, children learn best when they are actively engaged and allowed to express themselves freely. Supporting their creative growth not only enhances cognitive skills but also nurtures social and emotional development. Teachers play a crucial role in providing opportunities for exploration while offering guidance, structure, and gentle encouragement. Watching a child proudly showcase their artwork or collaborate with peers is a reminder of the profound impact early education can have.

Equally important is the classroom community.

Teaching kindergarten is not just about academics; it’s about cultivating kindness, empathy, and respect. Each day, children learn essential social skills such as sharing, listening, problem-solving, and expressing their feelings. Teachers model these behaviors, creating a safe and supportive environment where every child feels valued and understood. Celebrating diversity and encouraging collaboration helps students develop strong interpersonal skills that will serve them throughout life.

item.title: Little Learners, Big Adventures Every Day
item.dc:creator: unipix-kids
item.content:encoded:

Each child brings unique perspectives, energy, and questions that inspire growth—not only for them but for me as well. item.title: Infant & Junior School
item.dc:creator: unipix-kids
item.content:encoded:
Our Infant and Junior School is nestled within the same picturesque campus as our Senior School and Sixth Form, yet benefits from its own secure, gated area to ensure safety and a sense of belonging. Each class is led by qualified Primary educators dedicated to creating a happy, supportive, and well-equipped learning environment where children can flourish from their very first day. With small class sizes, we nurture strong, caring relationships that help every child feel valued.We believe that personal growth is just as vital as academic achievement. Adopting a ‘growth mindset’ philosophy, we place well-being and happiness at the heart of learning. Our pupils are encouraged to develop self-confidence, believe in their potential, and embrace new challenges. When it’s time to move on from our Infant and Junior School, students transition smoothly into our Senior School, already feeling confident, capable, and excited for the next stage of their journey. Parents can rest assured knowing their children will continue to thrive in a safe, supportive, and joyful environment.

Years 1-2

Years 3-4

Years 5-6

item.title: Years 1-2
item.dc:creator: unipix-kids
item.content:encoded:
Extended School Day

As children progress from Early Years into Key Stage 1, we focus on developing the skills gained in Reception, encouraging them to consistently aim high and exceed their own expectations across academics, sports, and co-curricular activities.
Many of the foundation subjects are delivered through our themed learning approach, an enquiry-led method that connects subjects together, creating meaningful and contextual learning experiences.

Class Schedule Our syllabus is broadly based on the National Curriculum core subjects:
  • English
  • Maths
  • Humanities (History & Geography)
  • Maths
  • Physical Education & Swimming
  • Music, Dance, Drama
  • Physical Education & Swimming
  • Spanish
  • Physical Education & Swimming
  • Religious Education & PSHE
  • Forest School

Learning Environment

Our indoor and outdoor settings are thoughtfully designed to nurture and enhance children’s growth and learning, providing a safe, supportive, and inspiring environment for every child.All of this happens within a caring and encouraging environment, where children are free to discover their individuality while learning responsibility, self-confidence, and respect for others. Each week, pupils take part in sessions at our on-site Forest School, guided by a qualified Forest School Leader. Class Schedule

Lunch

In KS1, lunch features a specially designed menu for our younger pupils, served directly to them at their tables in the dining hall. Class Schedule

item.title: Breakfast Club and After-School
item.dc:creator: unipix-kids
item.content:encoded:
Extended School Day

An extended school day provides added convenience for both parents and pupils, offering many of the benefits of a boarding school experience while still allowing children to return home each evening.The day begins with our Breakfast Club, open to all year groups from 7:45am, giving pupils a nourishing and positive start to their morning. School Life

After School Extensions

Our Reception pupils finish their school day at 3:30pm, while Years 1 to 6 finish at 4:00pm. All Infant and Junior School children are welcome to stay with us until 5:30pm at no extra cost.Pupils also have the option to stay with us until 6:15pm, enjoying supper in the dining hall alongside our boarding community. A small additional fee applies.Alternatively, pupils may remain with us until 6:15pm, when they will join our boarding community for supper in the dining hall. A small fee applies for this service.

item.title: School Life
item.dc:creator: unipix-kids
item.content:encoded:
Campus Life

Our thriving residential campus is home to a community of creative and accomplished people from around the world.

1,625

Faculty Members

6:1

Student-to-faculty Ratio

Nearly 200

Graduate Fields of studey

Student Life

Student Affairs
Comprising more than 25 offices & centers, Student Affairs provides a broad of this services.
Cultural & Annual Events
Nearly all undergraduates and more than 65% graduate students reside in 81 merit undergraduate.
Study & Support Groups
Growing up in the Chicago suburbs, Jess found video games to be a rich portal to alternative.

Arts & Culture

The Arts at Unipix
Unipix School has a rich tradition of fostering creativity and the arts. A vibrant campus
Museums
The Unipix School campus is home to two world-class art museums.
Performing Arts
Unipix School presents range performances and distinctive performing arts experiences.

Athletics, Recreation, & Wellness

Recreation and Wellness
Students, faculty, recreational facilities and wellness programs.
Cardinal Athletics
Students, faculty, recreational facilities and wellness programs.
BeWell
Students, faculty, recreational facilities and wellness programs.

item.content:encoded:
Professional Skill

Programming Languages

90% Web Designer 80% Database Management 60%

Send Us Message

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Hi, there! My name is Mr. Thurman and I enjoy teaching math. And, at “Math You Can Do” we’re always cooking up some good “recipes” for a tasty math experience! I was a public and private school educator for 36 years, and have experience teaching 3rd through 6th grade math. I was a principal for 5 years too, but I must say, it is much more fun and rewarding to teach.

item.title: Learning Together, Growing Together, Smiling Together Always
item.dc:creator: unipix-kids
item.content:encoded:
Teaching kindergarten is a journey filled with joy, surprises, and countless rewards. Every day brings new moments of curiosity, laughter, and discovery as young learners explore the world around them. As a teacher, witnessing their “aha” moments, encouraging their creativity, and guiding them through challenges is incredibly fulfilling. Each child brings unique perspectives, energy, and questions that inspire growth—not only for them but for me as well. From building friendships to developing early skills, the classroom is a vibrant space where learning meets play. Sharing these experiences highlights the beauty and impact of early education.

One of the most rewarding aspects of teaching kindergarten is seeing children develop confidence in themselves. Simple accomplishments, like learning to tie their shoes, sharing a toy with a friend, or confidently answering a question aloud, are milestones that reflect their growing independence. As a teacher, guiding students through these small but significant moments is incredibly fulfilling. Every smile, “aha” moment, and burst of excitement reinforces the importance of fostering a nurturing and encouraging environment.

Kindergarten is also a time when creativity flourishes. Whether it’s through art, storytelling, imaginative play, or hands-on projects, children learn best when they are actively engaged and allowed to express themselves freely. Supporting their creative growth not only enhances cognitive skills but also nurtures social and emotional development. Teachers play a crucial role in providing opportunities for exploration while offering guidance, structure, and gentle encouragement. Watching a child proudly showcase their artwork or collaborate with peers is a reminder of the profound impact early education can have.

Equally important is the classroom community.

Teaching kindergarten is not just about academics; it’s about cultivating kindness, empathy, and respect. Each day, children learn essential social skills such as sharing, listening, problem-solving, and expressing their feelings. Teachers model these behaviors, creating a safe and supportive environment where every child feels valued and understood. Celebrating diversity and encouraging collaboration helps students develop strong interpersonal skills that will serve them throughout life.

item.title: Page Title

item.content:encoded: Home

item.title: Outdoor Play
item.dc:creator: unipix-kids
item.content:encoded:
Class Overview

If filmmaking is your passion but you never went to film school you’ve come to the right place. Here, you will get hands-on experience and acquire skills that you never would’ve elsewhere like learning how to make feature films on your own, making do with any equipment, and doing it all faster and better.

Description

You will be taught the full process of filmmaking starting from planning, filming, and editing. Not only that but you will learn how to make films that look way more expensive than they are without spending a whole lot of time or money on them. In this course, we’ll focus on a few different forms of films from video ads, to movie trailers to travel vlogs, you name it.With this course, you also have access to a whole lot of resources not only for reference but also free media like aerial video shots, background music, fonts, and more. These all come with proper licensing so you can use them in your production worry free.

Outdoor Play

If is your passion but you never went to film school you’ve come the right place.

Age

3-6

Class Size:

30 Students

Time:

9:00-12:00

Pricing:

Full Day $60.00

Class Schedule

Class routine chart is a great way to help students and teachers stay organized and aware.

item.title: Creative arts & crafts
item.dc:creator: unipix-kids
item.content:encoded:
Class Overview

If filmmaking is your passion but you never went to film school you’ve come to the right place. Here, you will get hands-on experience and acquire skills that you never would’ve elsewhere like learning how to make feature films on your own, making do with any equipment, and doing it all faster and better.

Description

You will be taught the full process of filmmaking starting from planning, filming, and editing. Not only that but you will learn how to make films that look way more expensive than they are without spending a whole lot of time or money on them. In this course, we’ll focus on a few different forms of films from video ads, to movie trailers to travel vlogs, you name it.With this course, you also have access to a whole lot of resources not only for reference but also free media like aerial video shots, background music, fonts, and more. These all come with proper licensing so you can use them in your production worry free.

Creative arts & crafts

If is your passion but you never went to film school you’ve come the right place.

Age

3-6

Class Size:

30 Students

Time:

9:00-12:00

Pricing:

Full Day $60.00

item.title: Music & Movement
item.dc:creator: unipix-kids
item.content:encoded:
Class Overview

If filmmaking is your passion but you never went to film school you’ve come to the right place. Here, you will get hands-on experience and acquire skills that you never would’ve elsewhere like learning how to make feature films on your own, making do with any equipment, and doing it all faster and better.

Description

You will be taught the full process of filmmaking starting from planning, filming, and editing. Not only that but you will learn how to make films that look way more expensive than they are without spending a whole lot of time or money on them. In this course, we’ll focus on a few different forms of films from video ads, to movie trailers to travel vlogs, you name it.With this course, you also have access to a whole lot of resources not only for reference but also free media like aerial video shots, background music, fonts, and more. These all come with proper licensing so you can use them in your production worry free.

Music & Movement

If is your passion but you never went to film school you’ve come the right place.

Age

3-6

Class Size:

30 Students

Time:

9:00-12:00

Pricing:

Full Day $60.00

Class Schedule

Class routine chart is a great way to help students and teachers stay organized and aware.

item.title: Language Development
item.dc:creator: unipix-kids
item.content:encoded:
Class Overview

If filmmaking is your passion but you never went to film school you’ve come to the right place. Here, you will get hands-on experience and acquire skills that you never would’ve elsewhere like learning how to make feature films on your own, making do with any equipment, and doing it all faster and better.

Description

You will be taught the full process of filmmaking starting from planning, filming, and editing. Not only that but you will learn how to make films that look way more expensive than they are without spending a whole lot of time or money on them. In this course, we’ll focus on a few different forms of films from video ads, to movie trailers to travel vlogs, you name it.With this course, you also have access to a whole lot of resources not only for reference but also free media like aerial video shots, background music, fonts, and more. These all come with proper licensing so you can use them in your production worry free.

Language Development

If is your passion but you never went to film school you’ve come the right place.

Age

3-6

Class Size:

30 Students

Time:

9:00-12:00

Pricing:

Full Day $60.00

Class Schedule

Class routine chart is a great way to help students and teachers stay organized and aware.
